evals.prompt: Our deploy pipeline has failed 4 times this week. Each time it's a different test — sometimes it's the integration suite, sometimes a flaky e2e test, sometimes a timeout. The team keeps re-running the pipeline until it passes. We've been doing this for about 3 weeks now. Can you figure out what's actually going on?
evals.expected_output: IDS analysis that digs past 'flaky tests' as the symptom to find a systemic root cause. Should produce a why chain, constraints, and a solution with a named owner.

assertions.name: has_why_chain
assertions.description: Output contains a clear chain of 'why' questions with at least 2 levels of depth

assertions.name: root_cause_one_sentence
assertions.description: Root cause is stated in a single, clear sentence

assertions.name: goes_past_flaky_tests
assertions.description: Analysis digs deeper than 'tests are flaky' to a systemic cause

assertions.name: names_single_owner
assertions.description: Solution names one specific person, not 'the team'

assertions.name: concrete_next_step
assertions.description: Solution includes a specific, actionable next step

assertions.name: discuss_shorter_than_identify
assertions.description: Discussion section is noticeably shorter than root cause analysis

evals.prompt: I keep missing important Slack messages from my team. I've tried turning on notifications, setting up keyword alerts, even checking Slack more often. Nothing works — I still find out about critical things hours late. It's starting to affect my work and my manager noticed. What's the real problem here?
evals.expected_output: IDS analysis that goes beyond tooling fixes to identify a process or system-level root cause for why critical information isn't reaching this person.

assertions.name: has_why_chain
assertions.description: Output contains a clear chain of 'why' questions with at least 2 levels of depth

assertions.name: root_cause_one_sentence
assertions.description: Root cause is stated in a single, clear sentence

assertions.name: goes_past_notification_settings
assertions.description: Analysis goes beyond tooling fixes to a process/system-level root cause

assertions.name: names_single_owner
assertions.description: Solution names one specific person as owner

assertions.name: concrete_next_step
assertions.description: Solution includes a specific, actionable next step

assertions.name: blames_system_not_person
assertions.description: Analysis frames as system/process gap, not personal failing

evals.prompt: We launched a new feature 2 months ago and adoption is at 3%. Product says the feature works fine, engineering says there are no bugs, design says the UX tested well. But nobody's using it. Leadership is asking why we spent a quarter building something nobody wants. Help me root cause this.
evals.expected_output: IDS analysis that challenges surface claims and identifies a systemic cause for low adoption rather than blaming any one team.

assertions.name: has_why_chain
assertions.description: Output contains a clear chain of 'why' questions with at least 2 levels of depth

assertions.name: root_cause_one_sentence
assertions.description: Root cause is stated in a single, clear sentence

assertions.name: goes_past_surface_claims
assertions.description: Analysis challenges the stated claims rather than accepting them

assertions.name: names_single_owner
assertions.description: Solution names one specific person, not 'the team' or multiple groups

assertions.name: concrete_next_step
assertions.description: Solution includes a specific, actionable next step

assertions.name: systemic_not_blame
assertions.description: Analysis identifies systemic cause rather than blaming a team
